user_task: Multi-source synthesis: answer composed from multiple retrieved chunks across different documents.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE -> multi-source synthesis ('What do <A>'s, <B>'s, and <C>'s docs say about <topic>?'; 'Across all the docs I loaded, what's the most commonly mentioned <thing>?'; 'Across the memos in this folder, list every <item> and the total per project.').

success_criteria.quality: operational pass (NOT RAGAS): (1) retrieved hits span >= 2 DISTINCT source values (distinct documents), not all from one doc; (2) the final synthesized answer CITES EACH contributing source by name; (3) the answer COVERS ALL expected sources for the question -- an expected source that is loaded and relevant but missing from the answer is a PARTIAL FAIL counted against pass. Binary pass/fail per case; multi-source coverage is the defining gate (distinct from single-source RETRIEVE: this task REQUIRES synthesis across multiple source_ids).
success_criteria.time: RETRIEVE <= 1 min per case (one multi-doc ingest into a single table + one cross-doc query; non-agentic).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% -- a 'across all my docs / what do A's, B's, C's docs say' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% -- ingest the multi-doc corpus into ONE table (`retriever ingest <fileA> <fileB> [<fileC>]` or a folder), then `retriever query "<cross-doc q>" --top-k 8` with a top-k large enough to pull hits from multiple documents (optionally --rerank). Same --table-name on ingest and query. No --input-type flag (does not exist).

environment.host_agnostic: This RETRIEVE suite is host-agnostic: it asserts the cross-document synthesis behavior (hits from >=2 distinct sources, each cited, full coverage), not WHERE embedding/extraction ran. On a CPU/hosted box embedding/extraction default to hosted endpoints (NVIDIA_API_KEY); on a local GPU use the [local] backends (see the two SETUP suites). Defaults: lancedb_uri 'lancedb', table_name 'nemo-retriever'.
environment.multi_source_design: All cases ingest a 2-3 PDF corpus into ONE LanceDB table so a single query can retrieve across documents. The questions are deliberately constructed so the answer CANNOT be produced from any single document -- each contributing fact lives in a different PDF, forcing hits across multiple source values.

corpus_ground_truth.woods_frost.pdf: 2pg. p1: Robert Frost poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' ('His house is in the village though', 'And miles to go before I sleep'). p2: table 'Frost's Collections' (#, Collection, Year): A Boy's Will=1913, North of Boston=1914, Mountain Interval=1916, New Hampshire=1923, West Running Brook=1928.
corpus_ground_truth.table_test.pdf: 1pg. Numeric grid Year x {Bill, Amy, James, Ted, Susan}. James 2019 = 978; Susan 2023 = 970; Bill 2023 = 919.
corpus_ground_truth.multimodal_test.pdf: 3pg 'Testing Document'. Table 1 (Animal/Activity/Place): Giraffe/Driving a car/At the beach. Has 2 tables, 2 charts, 3 bullet points (per its own Conclusion, p3).
corpus_ground_truth.shared_theme: All three PDFs CONTAIN A TABLE (woods_frost p2 'Frost's Collections'; table_test numeric grid; multimodal_test Table 1 & Table 2). 'Tables' is therefore a verifiable cross-document common theme present in every loaded doc -- used by the 'most-commonly-mentioned / common theme' synthesis rungs.

environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int. 'source' is the per-hit document identifier used to count DISTINCT sources.
environment.operational_pass_note: Functional RETRIEVE uses OPERATIONAL pass, not RAGAS graded scoring. Pass = correct grounded answer + right subcommand/flags + the multi-source coverage gate (distinct sources, each cited, full coverage).

cases.title: 2-doc corpus: answer needs one fact from each doc -> hits span 2 distinct source values
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the RETRIEVE multi-source-synthesis core in its simplest form: a single query over a 2-PDF table whose answer needs a fact from EACH document, so retrieved hits MUST span >= 2 distinct source values. This is the multi-source floor and the one dimension that distinguishes this task from single-source RETRIEVE: the answer REQUIRES multiple source_ids. Adds nothing over a prior rung (it is the floor); coverage is trivially both docs.
cases.prompt: I loaded two PDFs into one folder. Across both data/woods_frost.pdf and data/table_test.pdf, answer in one go: which year did Robert Frost publish the collection 'New Hampshire', and what value did James have in 2019? Tell me which document each answer came from.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "Year Robert Frost published New Hampshire; James value in 2019" --top-k 8

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (both PDFs in one table; N >= 2)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; hits include at least one citing woods_frost.pdf AND at least one citing table_test.pdf
expected_output.answer: Frost published 'New Hampshire' in 1923 (woods_frost.pdf p2 'Frost's Collections' table); James had 978 in 2019 (table_test.pdf p1). Each fact attributed to its source document.
expected_output.multi_source_assertion: returned hits span >= 2 DISTINCT source values (woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf); the answer cites both

pass_when: both PDFs ingested into ONE table (same --table-name used by the query)
query uses --top-k 8 (large enough to pull hits from both docs)
retrieved hits span >= 2 DISTINCT source values (woods_frost.pdf AND table_test.pdf)
answer is exactly: 'New Hampshire' = 1923 (from woods_frost.pdf) AND James 2019 = 978 (from table_test.pdf)
answer attributes each fact to the correct source document

cases.title: Citation gate: every contributing source is named in the answer
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the validation clause 'the final answer CITES EACH contributing source'. Adds over rung 1 exactly one dimension: the grade now turns on explicit per-source CITATION, not just that hits happened to span two docs. Same 2-PDF corpus; the prompt explicitly demands a source label per fact, so an answer that fuses both facts without naming which doc each came from FAILS even if both facts are correct. New dimension: source attribution as a hard requirement.
cases.prompt: Using both data/woods_frost.pdf and data/table_test.pdf as my sources, give me two facts and CITE which file each came from: (a) one line of Robert Frost's poem, and (b) the value Susan had in 2023. For every fact, put the source filename in brackets after it.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "A line of Robert Frost's poem; Susan value in 2023" --top-k 8

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 2 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; hits citing woods_frost.pdf AND table_test.pdf
expected_output.answer: (a) a verbatim poem line such as 'And miles to go before I sleep' or 'His house is in the village though' [woods_frost.pdf]; (b) Susan 2023 = 970 [table_test.pdf]. Each fact carries its source filename in brackets.
expected_output.citation_assertion: EACH contributing source (woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf) is explicitly named next to the fact it supports

pass_when: hits span >= 2 distinct source values (woods_frost.pdf AND table_test.pdf)
answer cites BOTH sources explicitly, each next to the fact it supports (the citation gate)
fact (a) is a verbatim line of the Frost poem from woods_frost.pdf; fact (b) is Susan 2023 = 970 from table_test.pdf
FAILS if both facts are correct but the answer does not name which document each came from

cases.title: 3-doc corpus: 'most commonly mentioned / common theme' synthesis across ALL docs
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'Across all the docs I loaded, what's the most commonly mentioned <thing>?' seed query. Adds over rung 2: a THIRD document and a synthesis that is not a simple fact-per-doc lookup but an aggregation across the whole corpus -- the answer is the element common to ALL three docs (a table appears in every PDF), which can only be asserted by retrieving from all three. New dimension: corpus-wide aggregation / common-theme synthesis (not just one fact per doc).
cases.prompt: I loaded three PDFs into one folder: data/woods_frost.pdf, data/table_test.pdf, and data/multimodal_test.pdf. Across all the docs I loaded, what's a content element that every one of them contains? Name the element and point to where it appears in each document.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What content element (table) appears in every loaded document?" --top-k 8 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (all three PDFs in one table)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; hits span woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf AND multimodal_test.pdf
expected_output.answer: The common element is a TABLE: woods_frost.pdf p2 has the 'Frost's Collections' table; table_test.pdf p1 is itself a numeric Year x person grid; multimodal_test.pdf has Table 1 (Animal/Activity/Place) and Table 2 (car colors). A table appears in all three documents.
expected_output.multi_source_assertion: hits span >= 3 distinct source values (all three loaded docs); the common-theme answer is grounded in a hit from EACH

pass_when: all THREE PDFs ingested into ONE table
--top-k 8 query returns hits spanning >= 2 distinct sources (target: all 3 docs represented)
answer identifies 'a table' as the element common to all three documents
answer points to the table in EACH doc: Frost's Collections (woods_frost p2), the numeric grid (table_test p1), Table 1/Table 2 (multimodal_test)
synthesis is corpus-wide (aggregated across docs), not a single-doc lookup

cases.title: Coverage check: an expected source missing from the answer is a (partial) fail
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the validation clause 'the answer COVERS ALL expected sources (missing one expected source = partial fail counted against pass)'. Adds over rung 3: the grade now explicitly tests COVERAGE -- the prompt asks for a distinct fact from each of three named docs, and the case FAILS (partial) if any one expected source is absent from the answer, even if the present facts are correct and span >=2 sources. New dimension: full-coverage enforcement (the >=2-source floor is not enough; ALL expected sources must appear).
cases.prompt: I have exactly three documents loaded: data/woods_frost.pdf, data/table_test.pdf, and data/multimodal_test.pdf. List all three documents and give me one distinct fact from EACH: the earliest collection year from Frost's table, the value James had in 2019, and what activity the Giraffe is doing. I expect a fact from every document.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "earliest Frost collection year; James 2019 value; Giraffe activity" --top-k 8 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; hits must include woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf AND multimodal_test.pdf
expected_output.answer: Three documents loaded; one fact each: (1) woods_frost.pdf -> earliest Frost collection year = 1913 ('A Boy's Will', p2 table); (2) table_test.pdf -> James 2019 = 978 (p1); (3) multimodal_test.pdf -> Giraffe is 'Driving a car' (Table 1).
expected_output.coverage_assertion: ALL THREE expected sources are present in the answer; if any one (e.g. multimodal_test.pdf / the Giraffe fact) is missing, this is a PARTIAL FAIL even though the other two facts are correct and span 2 sources

pass_when: all three PDFs ingested into one table; --top-k 8 query
hits span all THREE distinct source values
answer lists all three documents AND gives one correct fact from EACH: 1913 (woods_frost), James 2019 = 978 (table_test), Giraffe 'Driving a car' (multimodal_test)
COVERAGE GATE: a missing expected source (any of the three absent from the answer) is a PARTIAL FAIL counted against pass -- the >=2-source floor alone does not pass this rung

cases.title: Acceptance: synthesis from >= 2 distinct docs, each cited, full expected-source coverage
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the multi-source-synthesis RETRIEVE row. Adds over rung 4: a single synthesis question that simultaneously exercises ALL three gates -- (a) hits span >= 2 distinct source values, (b) EACH contributing source is cited next to its fact, and (c) FULL coverage of all expected sources -- composed into one answer (a per-project-style roll-up listing each document and its distinct contributed fact). This is the gate the other rungs build up to; it is what 'multi-source synthesis' means end to end.
cases.prompt: Across all the memos in this folder (data/woods_frost.pdf, data/table_test.pdf, data/multimodal_test.pdf), produce a single synthesis: list every document, give one distinct fact pulled from each (Frost's most recent collection and its year; the value James had in 2019 from the grid; the place the Giraffe is in), and cite the source filename next to every fact. I want all three documents represented.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "Frost's most recent collection and year; James value in 2019; place the Giraffe is in" --top-k 8 --rerank --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; hits span woods_frost.pdf, table_test.pdf AND multimodal_test.pdf; --rerank orders cross-doc hits so all three surface in top-k 8
expected_output.answer: Synthesis listing all three docs with one distinct cited fact each: woods_frost.pdf -> most recent collection 'West Running Brook' (1928) [woods_frost.pdf, p2]; table_test.pdf -> James 2019 = 978 [table_test.pdf, p1]; multimodal_test.pdf -> the Giraffe is 'At the beach' (Table 1) [multimodal_test.pdf].
expected_output.acceptance_assertion: all three gates pass at once -- hits span >= 2 (here 3) distinct sources, each source is cited next to its fact, and all three expected sources are covered

pass_when: all three PDFs ingested into one table; --top-k 8 with --rerank so cross-doc hits all surface
retrieved hits span >= 2 DISTINCT source values (here all 3)
answer cites EACH contributing source filename next to its fact (citation gate)
FULL coverage: all three documents appear in the answer (coverage gate) -- a missing source is a partial fail
facts grounded: West Running Brook = 1928 (woods_frost p2); James 2019 = 978 (table_test p1); Giraffe 'At the beach' (multimodal_test Table 1)
this rung passes only if distinct-sources + each-cited + full-coverage ALL hold in one synthesized answer
cases.known_caveat: All three contributed facts are point lookups grounded in verbatim fixture content (woods_frost p2 'West Running Brook 1928'; table_test p1 'James 2019 = 978'; multimodal_test Table 1 'Giraffe / At the beach') so the answer does not depend on scanning or aggregating the full grid. The multi-source gates (distinct sources, each cited, full coverage) are what this rung is built to enforce, independent of the specific facts chosen.
